text.title: Positioning Diff Log Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Positioning Diff Log. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every positioning wording change creates exactly one log entry. Skipped changes destroy traceability.
rule.rationale: Skipped changes destroy traceability + ability to roll back.

rule.statement: Entry names the trigger: sales call, churn signal, competitor move, founder hunch. 'Iteration' is not a trigger.
rule.rationale: Trigger-less entries cannot be evaluated as good/bad calls.

rule.statement: Old wording AND new wording captured verbatim. Paraphrases lose the diff.
rule.rationale: Paraphrased versions hide the actual change.

rule.statement: Expected effect names a metric + direction + magnitude (e.g. trial signup CVR +20% in 30 days). Vague effects cannot be checked.
rule.rationale: Vague effects cannot be checked; positioning drifts unchecked.

rule.statement: Schedule 30-day + 90-day check on the named metric. Result captured in the same entry. No retro = no learning.
rule.rationale: Without retro the positioning drifts unchecked.

rule.statement: Owner is named (founder counts); 'team' / 'we' not allowed.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership drifts to anonymous changes.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-positioning-diff-log.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
